content: {"queries_executed": [{"purpose": "Count planets by discovery method with radius and period ranges", "sql": "SELECT discoverymethod, COUNT(*) as cnt, MIN(pl_rade) as min_radius, MAX(pl_rade) as max_radius, MIN(pl_orbper) as min_period, MAX(pl_orbper) as max_period, SUM(CASE WHEN pl_rade IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as radius_nulls FROM dataset GROUP BY discoverymethod ORDER BY cnt DESC", "key_results": "Transit: 68 planets (0.85-15.0 Re, 0.73-8.32 days), RV: 18 (1.88-13.5 Re, 0.74-5218 days), Imaging: 7 (12.0-14.5 Re, 7890-320000 days)"}, {"purpose": "Check NULL rates for key columns by method", "sql": "SELECT discoverymethod, COUNT(*) as total, SUM(CASE WHEN pl_rade IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as rade_null, SUM(CASE WHEN pl_bmasse IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as mass_null, SUM(CASE WHEN pl_orbeccen IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as ecc_null FROM dataset GROUP BY discoverymethod", "key_results": "Transit has lowest NULL rate for radius (5%), RV has lowest for mass (17%), Imaging has 0% NULL for radius but no eccentricity data"}], "summary": "Transit-discovered planets dominate the sample (68%) and cluster at short orbital periods (all < 9 days) with radii from 0.85 to 15 Earth radii. Radial velocity detections span a much wider period range (up to 5218 days) but have higher radius NULL rates. Imaging finds only distant giants with periods of thousands to hundreds of thousands of days. This confirms strong method-dependent selection effects that must be accounted for in any cross-method analysis.", "details": "NULL rates vary systematically by method: Transit has the best radius coverage (95% complete) but worse mass coverage. RV has better mass completeness. Imaging targets are all large (>12 Re) by construction. Microlensing and Pulsar Timing contribute only 5 planets total with sparse property measurements.", "view_requested": null}
